This document outlines the interconnected dependencies and implementation phases required to deliver Pure’s journey mapping recommendation engine and brand refresh. It incorporates CTA orchestration, sitemap optimization, configurator strategy, page simplification, personalization, design system updates, and asset production practices. Each element ties back to our guiding principles: simplicity, clarity, modularity, and persona-driven orchestration.

other.text: We’re back after our On-Demand break, and will be walking through the team’s design initiatives and aligning on our testing agenda.
We’ll walk through our testing plan including:
Previous A/B testing has revealed a 375% increase in conversion by shifting CTA labels from Test Drive to “Try Now”.
The team would like to outline key terms for needfinding and understanding the current market perception of common ‘demo’ terms.
We’ve got the goods on how we can elevate Test Drive content and validates our hunches around placing Customer content in the nav.
We’ll review the results of our Partner page updates and

other.text: We’ll review previous year’s testing and insights into the Accelerate web promotions. This will gives us an overarching perspective to identify areas to highlight this year.

Takeaways - A teaser that tells users what you’ll be telling them upon release will build more anticipation. They’re already curious about the details of the release, specs, what will be communicated, and making these promises up front will build excitement.
→ Removing information reduced comprehension (9%) but boosted sentiment (+5%) and encouraged more user actions (+3%).
Feedback : Build-out for stakeholder presentations would be greatly helped by legends and clarifications of how to interpret data presentation.
Intercepts → working with the team to get some answers
Next Steps → ZURB owes PS a response.
→ Page was seen as a product and services overview for Pure Storage.
→ Pure//Launch and Pure//Updates labels both match the page’s content.
→ Most participants (63%) would look under Products to find Pure//Launch.
Dive in deeper with naming , with only ~30% affinity to Pure//launch or Pure//Updates labeling how can we improve the association to all updates of Pure?
Place page within Products since this is a product launch page.
Market Pure//Launch similar to product pages, since this is page is seen as a overview page for all products.

children.text: 136 - TCO Calculator - We identified quite a few areas to improve the design of the TCO calculators, including clarifying the savings calculation, pulling the total calculations more closely to the charts, and ensuring interactive elements are consistently and clearly outlined.
137 - NVIDIA GTC Event - We walked through various insights from our testing of the NVIDIA flow, including opportunities to clarify "Add to Calendar" functionality and ideal headlines.
138 - Events Center - We see that the Series module is rated least valuable on the page. We'll run follow up testing to see what affects we might expect from removing it.

139 - Marquee Messaging - The team saw a disconnect between the visual imagery, which presented as symbolic and negative, and the purpose behind the messaging in the modules. The darker modules also saw a higher engagement rate.

other.text: Comprehension : Changing the story slightly improved comprehension by 2% compared to the previous version but still fell short of the clarity achieved with the side navigation. Resource sections, such as competitor pages, company details, and blogs, were found to be more helpful in improving understanding.

Sentiment : The story change negatively impacted sentiment. 22% of participants described the page as complicated, and 10% found it confusing. Positive impressions—such as being transparent, approachable, trustworthy, and confident—also dropped noticeably.
ZURB provides weekly concept testing, additional feature support for the experience and functionality, and web analysis.
